result:
All changes are in place and verified with a real `next build` + local server test. Here's what was wrong and what I fixed:

**Root cause**: this site is mid-pivot. It rebranded from "InboxZap" (an email-cleanup tool) to "Lumina" (an AI meeting-notes assistant), but the technical SEO surface never caught up — the homepage content talks about Lumina while everything crawlers/search results see about the site still pointed at the old product or was simply broken.

**Fixed:**
1. **Title/meta/OG tag mismatch** (`app/layout.tsx`) — every page was serving the old "InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes" title and description while the actual homepage is about Lumina. This is likely the single biggest suppressor of rankings/CTR: Google devalues pages where the promise in the title doesn't match the content. Rewrote to match the real product, added `metadataBase` and canonical URL.
2. **Broken OG image** — metadata referenced `/img/og-inboxzap.png`, which doesn't exist anywhere in `public/`. Replaced with a real generated image via `app/opengraph-image.tsx`.
3. **Dead nav links** — `/features` and `/pricing` were linked in the header nav but had no pages (404s). Built both out.
4. **Broken FAQ redirect** — `/faq` permanently redirects to `/#faq`, but no `id="faq"` existed anywhere. Added a real FAQ section to the homepage.
5. **Blog was non-functional for SEO** — `app/blog/page.tsx` was a client component fetching from `/api/posts`, which doesn't exist in the repo (shows a permanent loading spinner to crawlers that don't execute the fetch, and no individual post routes existed at all). Converted to server-rendered, added `app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx` with per-post metadata, and wrote 3 posts on-topic for the current product (the old GSC-indexed posts were about email cleanup — off-topic now).
6. **Sitemap excluded all blog content** (explicit TODO) despite those pages already being indexed with real impression volume in GSC. Now includes `/features`, `/pricing`, and every blog post.
7. **Stale indexed URLs** — GSC shows `/blog/post-1` through `/post-12` (old InboxZap-topic posts) still getting thousands of impressions at page-4/5 rankings. Since that content no longer exists, added 301 redirects from those URLs to `/blog` instead of leaving them to soft-404, to recover some of that equity rather than waste it.

One thing worth flagging: `npm install` reported next@14.2.5 has a known security vulnerability (patch available) — outside this task's scope, but worth a separate upgrade at some point.
